sourceMovie.title: Warrior
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Warrior works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Creed III
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Warrior clicked for you, Creed III (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Warrior clicked for you, Never Back Down (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Warrior?
faq.answer: Try Creed III, Never Back Down, Bloodsport—each shares DNA with Warrior while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Warrior?
faq.answer: Warrior (2011) is best described as Drama, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Warrior worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
